title: Postpartum Weight Loss Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate realistic postpartum weight loss timeline. Enter your pre-pregnancy weight, current weight, and breastfeeding status. Free & fast.
seoKeywords: how long to lose baby weight
how much weight do you lose after delivery
when do you lose postpartum weight
intro: You lose roughly **5–6 kg** immediately after delivery (baby, placenta, amniotic fluid). The rest of pregnancy weight takes **6–12 months** to lose healthily. Get a **realistic timeline** based on your situation.
keyTakeaway: You lose **~5–6 kg** right after birth. The rest takes **6–12 months** at a healthy rate of **0.5 kg/week**. Breastfeeding helps (~500 extra kcal/day). **Avoid restrictive diets** postpartum.
useCases: Want to know how long it'll take to return to your pre-pregnancy weight.
Looking for a realistic postpartum weight loss plan.
Frustrated by slow weight loss and need realistic expectations.
Wondering if breastfeeding helps with weight loss.
Your doctor recommended weight loss and you need a realistic timeline.

outputs.label: Estimated time to reach pre-pregnancy weight

example.title: Example: Pre-pregnancy 62 kg, current 72 kg, 4 weeks postpartum, breastfeeding
example.result: You have **10 kg** to lose to return to your pre-pregnancy weight. At a healthy rate of **0.5 kg/week**, you could reach it in ~**5 months**. Breastfeeding gives you a calorie advantage.

explanation:
## Postpartum Weight Loss Timeline

| Stage | Expected Weight Loss |
|---|---|
| Immediate delivery | 5–6 kg (baby, placenta, fluid) |
| Weeks 1–2 | 2–3 kg extra (fluid retention) |
| Weeks 2–6 | Variable (hormonal adjustment) |
| Months 2–6 | 0.5 kg/week with healthy eating |
| Months 6–12 | Last kilos are often the hardest |

## Does Breastfeeding Help Weight Loss?

Yes. Milk production burns **~500 kcal/day**. Many mothers naturally lose weight while breastfeeding. However, some retain weight during lactation due to hormonal factors (prolactin can promote fat storage).

## Healthy Postpartum Weight Loss Tips

- **Avoid restrictive diets** (minimum 1,800 kcal if breastfeeding)
- **Moderate deficit**: 300–500 kcal below your needs
- **Gradual activity**: walking, then progressive exercise
- **Be patient**: your body took 9 months to change—give it at least that long
- **Prioritize sleep** (hard with a newborn, but sleep affects metabolism)

## Related Calculators

- [Breastfeeding Calories](/calculadora-calorias-lactancia) — how much to eat.
- [Postpartum Recovery](/calculadora-posparto-recuperacion) — recovery timeline.
- [Postpartum Depression Screening](/calculadora-depresion-posparto-test) — mental health.

faq.q: How much weight do you lose immediately after giving birth?
faq.a: You lose **5–6 kg** right away: baby (~3.3 kg), placenta (~0.7 kg), amniotic fluid (~0.8 kg), blood (~0.5 kg). In the first 2 weeks, you'll lose another **2–3 kg** of fluid retention.

faq.q: How long does it take to lose postpartum weight?
faq.a: Most mothers return to their pre-pregnancy weight in **6–12 months**. Some take longer, especially retaining the final 2–3 kg. Not everyone reaches their exact pre-pregnancy weight, and that's normal.
A **moderate deficit** (300–500 kcal) is safe while breastfeeding. Don't drop below **1,800 kcal/day**. Very restrictive diets reduce milk supply and your already-low energy with a newborn.

faq.q: Why am I not losing weight while breastfeeding?
faq.a: Some mothers retain weight during lactation due to **prolactin** (the hormone that stimulates milk production, but can also promote fat storage). Final kilos often shed after weaning.

faq.q: When can I start exercising after delivery?
faq.a: **Walking**: weeks 1–2. **Moderate exercise**: week 6 with doctor clearance. **After C-section**: wait 8–10 weeks. Start gradually and listen to your body.

faq.q: Is it normal to weigh more than before pregnancy one year later?
faq.a: Many mothers retain **1–3 kg** above their pre-pregnancy weight, especially after their first baby. Bodies change (composition, hormones, lifestyle). If concerned, see a nutritionist.

faq.q: How many calories does breastfeeding burn per day?
faq.a: Breastfeeding burns approximately **500 kcal/day**, though this varies based on milk supply and frequency. Exclusive breastfeeding burns more than mixed feeding.

faq.q: Do postpartum belly bands or girdles help you lose weight?
faq.a: Belly bands provide **comfort and support**, but they don't burn fat or reduce belly size. Your belly naturally contracts as the uterus shrinks (6–8 weeks), then core exercise and time finish the job.

sources.name: ACOG — Weight Gain During Pregnancy and Postpartum Weight Loss
